title: Anal fissure symptoms

red_flags.text: Supports pain provoked by defecation, pain after defecation, bright-red bleeding, constipation or diarrhoea context, and duration.
red_flags.rationale: The exact presentation section supports defecation-related pain, post-defecation pain, bright-red bleeding, constipation or diarrhoea context, duration, and impact without diagnosing a fissure. The exact section supports recording already reviewed investigation context and clinician-entered follow-up without generating examination, treatment, or referral.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports pain provoked by defecation, pain after defecation, bright-red bleeding, constipation or diarrhoea context, and duration.

source.title: The American Society of Colon and Rectal Surgeons Clinical Practice Guidelines for the Management of Anal Fissures

exact_section.heading: Statement of the problem — presentation and symptom pattern

examination.text: Supports documenting clinician-observed location, multiplicity, chronicity features, and context requiring broader assessment without asserting a cause.
examination.rationale: The exact atypical-context section supports clinician-observed anorectal findings and concern documentation only when assessed; it does not imply an intimate examination occurred.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports documenting clinician-observed location, multiplicity, chronicity features, and context requiring broader assessment without asserting a cause.

source.title: The American Society of Colon and Rectal Surgeons Clinical Practice Guidelines for the Management of Anal Fissures

source_limitations: Exact bleeding amount, bowel pattern, pregnancy or postpartum status, consent, chaperone, examination findings, diagnosis, medicine, procedure, referral, and urgency remain unsupported unless entered.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
